howItWorks.intro: This hearing loss frequency calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Max Frequency
rules.explanation: Age-related decline plus noise exposure damage.

howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.q: What frequency range is normal hearing?
faq.a: 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz for young adults. High frequencies decline naturally with age.
At high volumes (over 85 dB), extended earbud use can accelerate hearing loss.

howWeCalculate.intro: This hearing loss frequency calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.
steps: Enter your specific values into the calculator fields above

Results are calculated instantly in your browser — nothing is sent to a server
